text: This is endoclinch, a tissue retrieval bag by the Covidien.
This is very important if the gallbladder is inflamed and if you have suspicion of malignancy, you should use endoclinch and you can close it nicely.
The endoclinch tissue retrieval bag is shown with its self-retaining metal ring, designed to securely hold tissue during the cholecystectomy procedure.
The surgeon closes the endoclinch tissue retrieval bag and pulls it into the cannula for secure removal.
The surgeon uses ovum forceps to crush a large gallstone, then carefully stretches the abdominal wall to slowly extract the fragments.
Endoclinch will not allow the contamination or the malignancy to be transferred to the abdominal wall.
